rationale: The page provides a detailed comparison between the Fifth and Sixth Schedules of the Indian Constitution regarding tribal administration. It also outlines the broader tribal governance framework and recent government initiatives like PM-JANMAN and the Adi Karmayogi Abhiyan, which are highly relevant for polity and social issues exams.
cleanedText:
Difference between Fifth and Sixth Schedule
| Parameter | Fifth schedule | Sixth Schedule |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Applicability | Certain Scheduled Areas declared by President in any state of India. (Currently in 10 states) | Tribal areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram. |
| Administration Body | Tribes Advisory Councils (TAC) | ADCs & ARCs |
| Powers | TAC being the creation of Legislative Assembly of States has limited powers which are mostly executive. | Extensive powers, including executive, legislative and judicial powers, all derived from the Constitution. |
| PESA Act 1996 | Applicable | Not applicable |
Other Tribal Governance Framework
- Ministry of Tribal Affairs (1999): Nodal ministry for integrated socio-economic development of Scheduled Tribes.
- PESA Act, 1996: Empowers Gram Sabhas in Scheduled Areas.
- Authority over natural resources, land transfers, and local development.
- Customary Tribal Institutions: Adivasi Sabhas/Tribal Panchayats manage culture, resources, and dispute resolution alongside formal Panchayats.
Major Steps towards Tribal Development
- Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan, 2023-24: Focused development of 75 PVTGs across 18 states & 1 UT.
- The mission aims to provide basic facilities such as safe housing, clean drinking water and improved access to education, health and nutrition, etc in 3 years.
- These objectives are planned to be met through 11 interventions implemented by 9 Ministries.
- Dharti Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan (DAJGUA): Infrastructure & socio-economic upliftment in tribal-majority villages with tribal population of 500 or more and at least 50% tribal residents as well as villages in Aspirational Districts with a tribal population of 50 or more.
- Development Action Plan for Scheduled Tribes: 41 ministries earmark funds annually for tribal development.
- PM Adi Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAAGY): Model village development covering ~40% tribal population.
- Entrepreneurship Promotion: Van Dhan Vikas Kendras, PM Janjatiya Vikas Mission, TRIFED/Tribes India outlets.
8.7.1. ADI KARMAYOGI
Why in the News?
Special Gram Sabhas held across 1 lakh tribal-dominated villages and Tolas adopted the Tribal Village Vision 2030 Declaration as part of Adi Karmayogi Abhiyan.
